view.s1297.cons.mtm: § 1296 MTM: mark-to-market on tradable PFIC stock
view.s1297.cons.qef: § 1295 QEF election: annual current inclusion + preserve character
view.s1297.cons.taint: 'Once PFIC, always PFIC' until purged via deemed sale
view.s1297.exc.5pct_passive: 5% de minimis: ≤ 5% income test exception in cleansing years
view.s1297.exc.banking: § 1297(b)(2)(A) active banking: ≥ 4 financial professionals + customary loans + deposits
view.s1297.exc.cfc_overlap: § 1298(b)(8) CFC overlap: CFC US 10% shareholder NOT PFIC for that shareholder
view.s1297.exc.commodities: Active commodities dealer: ≥ 75% income from regular trade dealer activity
view.s1297.exc.foreign_personal: Foreign personal services: NOT passive if performed by service-providers
view.s1297.exc.related_party: Related-party rent / royalty: NOT passive (look-through to active business)
view.s1297.exc.startup: § 1298(b)(2) STARTUP: first year of new active business may escape PFIC if not PFIC in first 3 yrs
view.s1297.h1.title: // § 1297 PFIC DEFINITION
view.s1297.hint.intro: PFIC if EITHER: (1) 75%+ income passive OR (2) 50%+ assets produce passive income (or held for such). 'Once a PFIC, always a PFIC' in shareholder's hands until purged. Look-through rule: ≥ 25%-owned subsidiaries taxed on look-through basis. Startup exception § 1298(b)(2): first year of new corp may be exempt. CFC overlap: CFC US shareholders escape PFIC regime under § 1298(b)(8). Form 8621 annually for each PFIC.
view.s1297.lt.allocation: Pro-rata share of sub's income + assets allocated to parent
view.s1297.lt.dividend_excl: Dividends from sub: NOT counted as passive income (avoid double-counting)
view.s1297.lt.intercompany: Intercompany debt: special rules to prevent abuse
view.s1297.lt.intermediate: Multi-tier: look-through cascades through chain of 25%+ owned subs
view.s1297.lt.measurement: Asset measurement: FMV at end of each quarter, averaged
view.s1297.lt.passive_assets_active: Foreign sub's active assets cleanse parent's passive characterization
view.s1297.lt.threshold: ≥ 25% owned subsidiaries treated as look-through (income + asset basis)
view.s1297.tests.assets: ASSET test: 50%+ of FMV avg assets produce passive income / held for such
view.s1297.tests.banking: Active banking exception: ≥ 4 financial professionals, regulatory oversight
view.s1297.tests.commodities: Commodities trading: special active dealer exception
view.s1297.tests.either: EITHER triggers PFIC — not both required
view.s1297.tests.income: INCOME test: 75%+ of gross income is 'passive' (§ 1297(b))
view.s1297.tests.passive_def: Passive: interest, dividends, royalties, rents, net gains on personal property
view.s1297.tests.real_estate: Rental income: passive UNLESS active real estate trade or business
view.s1298.att.cooperative: Cooperative ownership rules: aggregate indirect interests
view.s1298.att.foreign_partnership: Foreign partnership: still PFIC pass-through to US owners
view.s1298.att.grantor: Grantor trust: deemed owned by grantor for PFIC purposes
view.s1298.att.partnership: Partnerships: pass through to partners pro-rata
view.s1298.att.tiered: Tiered structure: cascade through all layers
view.s1298.att.trust: Trusts: pass to beneficiaries by interest
view.s1298.cfc.dual_residency: Dual residency exception: treaty-resident considered foreign for PFIC
view.s1298.cfc.form_5471: Form 5471 reporting replaces Form 8621 PFIC reporting
view.s1298.cfc.policy: Policy: subF + GILTI already address tax abuse; no double-rule
view.s1298.cfc.s962_election: § 962 election available for individual US 10% shareholders
view.s1298.cfc.smaller_shareholder: Other US shareholders (< 10%) still subject to PFIC
view.s1298.cfc.test: US 10% shareholder of CFC: NOT subject to PFIC regime
view.s1298.cfc.test_when_no_longer: If US ownership drops below 10% → PFIC regime triggers
view.s1298.h1.title: // § 1298 PFIC SPECIAL RULES
view.s1298.hint.intro: § 1298(a) Attribution: PFIC stock owned through partnerships, S-corps, trusts, estates flows to ultimate US owner. § 1298(b)(1) 'Once PFIC, always PFIC': taint persists in shareholder's hands; cured only by PURGING (deemed sale at § 1291 rates). § 1298(b)(2) Startup: first year of new corp may be exempt if not PFIC in first 3 years. § 1298(b)(8) CFC overlap: CFC US 10% shareholders ESCAPE PFIC regime. § 1298(f) Form 8621 reporting requirement.
view.s1298.rep.failure: Failure to file: $10K minimum + 25% understatement penalty
view.s1298.rep.partner_share: Partners + S-corp shareholders file own Form 8621
view.s1298.rep.per_pfic: Form 8621 PER PFIC each year (not aggregate)
view.s1298.rep.required: Any US person owning PFIC interest (direct or indirect) MUST file Form 8621
view.s1298.rep.statute_extension: Statute of limitations extends 3 yrs after filing
view.s1298.rep.threshold: Reportable: aggregate value > $25K ($50K MFJ) OR Pedigree Election Made
view.s1298.rep.trustees: Grantor trust trustees file on behalf of grantor
view.s1298.report_note: Form 8621 REQUIRED but NOT FILED. Penalty: $10K minimum + 25% understatement penalty + statute of limitations 3-yr extension. Streamlined Filing Compliance Procedures (SFCP) may apply for delinquent reporting. Foreign mutual funds + ETFs almost always PFICs → annual filing per fund.
view.s1298.start.cash_intensive: Cash-intensive R&D stage: typically PFIC despite eventual active business
view.s1298.start.failed_acquirer: Failed acquirer: holding-co structure may inadvertently trigger
view.s1298.start.first_year: FIRST YEAR of new active business corp
view.s1298.start.no_active_business: No active business → not eligible for startup exception
view.s1298.start.pre_operating: Pre-operating phase common (no income but high assets)
view.s1298.start.test: Test: not PFIC in any of first 3 yrs
view.s1298.start.year_1: If passes 3-yr test → year 1 retroactively NOT PFIC
view.s1298.taint.basic: § 1298(b)(1): foreign corp PFIC for any year in shareholder's hands = PFIC for ALL subsequent years
view.s1298.taint.cleansing_election: § 1298(b)(1)(B) cleansing election: deemed sale at § 1291 rates → cleanse
view.s1298.taint.corp_changes: Even if corp changes operations + ceases to be PFIC under tests, taint persists to that shareholder
view.s1298.taint.death: Death of shareholder: § 1014 step-up + restart from heirs' perspective
view.s1298.taint.disposition_cleanses: Complete disposition (sale + 36 months pass): may cleanse
view.s1298.taint.purge_deemed_sale: Purge via DEEMED SALE election: recognize gain currently at § 1291 rates → restart clean
view.s1298.taint.purge_mtm_late: MTM election (after PFIC status): apply purging gain recognition
view.s1298.taint.purge_qef_late: Late QEF election: also requires PURGING via deemed sale
view.s132.cat.athletic: (a)(8) Athletic Facility: on-premises gym, mostly used by employees
view.s132.cat.de_minimis: (a)(4) De Minimis: occasional snacks, supper money, holiday gifts < $100
view.s132.cat.discount: (a)(2) Qualified Discount: ≤ employer gross profit % (or 20% for services)
view.s132.cat.no_add_cost: (a)(1) No-Additional-Cost: airline space-available, hotel rooms — no employer marginal cost
view.s132.cat.working: (a)(3) Working Condition: laptop, magazine subscription, professional dues
view.s132.dm.flowers: Flowers / fruit for special events (achievement, illness)
view.s132.dm.gift_cards: CASH or CASH EQUIVALENT (gift cards) NEVER de minimis
view.s132.h1.title: // § 132 STATUTORY FRINGE BENEFITS
view.s132.hint.intro: 8 categories excluded from employee income: (a)(1) No-Additional-Cost, (a)(2) Qualified Employee Discount, (a)(3) Working Condition, (a)(4) De Minimis, (a)(5) Qualified Transportation ($315/mo 2024), (a)(6) Qualified Moving (military only), (a)(7) Retirement Planning, (a)(8) Athletic Facility. Non-discrim rules limit highly-compensated. TCJA 2018 eliminated employer deduction for qualified transportation.
view.s132.tr.combine: Cannot combine: transit + parking allowed but separate limits
view.s132.tr.no_deduct: Employer can't DEDUCT qualified transportation expense (TCJA repeal)
view.s132.tr.salary_reduction: Pre-tax salary reduction allowed (employee pays less FICA + income tax)
view.s134.h1.title: // § 134 MILITARY BENEFITS EXCLUSION
view.s134.hint.intro: Qualified military benefits + § 112 combat zone pay exclusion (enlisted full / officer capped). § 121(d)(9) 10-yr home sale ext + § 7508 SOL ext.
view.s1361.card.se_saved: SE tax saved vs Schedule C
view.s1361.case.davis: Davis v. United States (1976, 11th Cir.) — established multi-factor framework
view.s1361.case.fact_sheet: IRS Fact Sheet 2008-25: 9 factors for reasonable comp determination
view.s1361.case.glass_blocks: Glass Blocks Unlimited v. Comm'r (2013) — DENIED $30k on $700k revenue
view.s1361.case.sean: Sean McAlary Ltd. v. Comm'r (2013) — DENIED $24k for real estate agent; reclassified $83k
view.s1361.case.watson: Watson, P.C. v. United States (8th Cir. 2012) — DENIED $24k on $200k+ profit; reclassified $91k as wages
view.s1361.elect.consent: All shareholders must sign / spouse must consent if community property
view.s1361.elect.deadline: Form 2553 due by Mar 15 of effective year (calendar) OR within 2 mo 15 days of inception
view.s1361.elect.eligible: Eligible: 100 shareholder cap, 1 class of stock, no foreign owners, no C-corp/LLC owners
view.s1361.elect.late: Late election: Rev. Proc. 2013-30 auto-relief if < 3 yr 75 days + reasonable cause
view.s1361.elect.tax_year: Calendar year required unless § 444 election + back-up payment
view.s1361.elect.trusts: Trust shareholders: ESBT or QSST election required
view.s1361.h1.title: // § 1361 S-CORP + REASONABLE COMP
view.s1361.hint.intro: Form 2553 election: 2 months 15 days into election year (Mar 15 for calendar). LATE election? Rev. Proc. 2013-30 auto-relief if reasonable cause + within 3-yr 75-day window. Reasonable Compensation: W-2 wages must be arms-length BEFORE distributions. Saves SE tax (15.3%) on profit ABOVE reasonable comp. Audit risk: Watson 8th Cir. lost $24k comp on $200k profit case.
view.s1362.es.esbt: Electing Small Business Trust (ESBT) — multi-beneficiary US persons
view.s1362.es.grantor_trust: Grantor trust (during grantor's lifetime + 2 yrs after death)
view.s1362.es.iras: IRA shareholders (post-2017 for bank S-corps only)
view.s1362.es.no_corp: NOT eligible: corp, partnership, foreign trust, NRA, multi-beneficiary trust w/o ESBT
view.s1362.es.qsst: Qualified Subchapter S Trust (QSST) — single beneficiary US person
view.s1362.h1.title: // § 1362 S-CORP ELECTION
view.s1362.hint.intro: Form 2553 filed by 15th day of 3rd month of tax year for election that year. ALL shareholders must consent in writing. Termination triggers: revocation, > 100 shareholders, ineligible shareholder, > 25% passive income (3 consecutive yrs with C-corp E&P). Late election relief: Rev. Proc. 2013-30 + Rev. Proc. 2022-19 — relief often available within 3 yrs 75 days. 5-year post-termination wait before re-electing (unless IRS consent). Inadvertent termination relief § 1362(f) — taxpayer + IRS in tandem.
view.s1362.label.classes: More than one class of stock?
view.s1362.lr.2022_19: Rev. Proc. 2022-19: updated guidance + expanded scope for common errors
view.s1362.lr.late_filing: Late filing: attach Form 2553 to first late return + Rev. Proc. reference
view.s1362.lr.plr_alternative: PLR alternative: ~$30K+ fee + 6-9 months wait if outside automatic relief
view.s1362.lr.purpose: Cure missed election + missed terminating events without PLR
view.s1362.lr.requirements: Requirements: reasonable cause, return filed consistent w/ S, statement of facts
view.s1362.lr.scope: Scope: S-corp election, ESBT, QSST, QSub elections
view.s1362.lr.timing: Generally must request within 3 years 75 days of intended effective date
view.s1362.lr.user_fee: No user fee — free relief if timely
view.s1362.mech.confirmation: IRS confirmation: CP261 notice (acceptance) within 60 days
view.s1362.mech.deadline: By 15th day of 3rd month of tax year (March 15 for calendar)
view.s1362.mech.entity_class: Entity must be eligible S corp: domestic, ≤ 100 shareholders, no ineligible, 1 class of stock
view.s1362.mech.late_75: 75-day late election available with reasonable cause
view.s1362.mech.no_form_filed: If no CP261: contact IRS — common error
view.s1362.mech.qsst_esbt: QSST / ESBT trust elections: required to be eligible shareholders
view.s1362.mech.signatures: ALL shareholders + spouses (community property states) must sign
view.s1362.mech.tax_year: Choose acceptable tax year (calendar OR § 444 fiscal w/ § 7519 deposit)
view.s1362.term.5_year_wait: 5-year wait before re-election (IRS consent required to shorten)
view.s1362.term.classes: 2+ classes of stock (voting / non-voting OK; different distribution rights NOT)
view.s1362.term.inadvertent_relief: Inadvertent termination: § 1362(f) relief if cured promptly
view.s1362.term.ineligible: Ineligible shareholder (corp, partnership, NRA, non-grantor trust)
view.s1362.term.passive_income: > 25% passive income 3 consecutive years + has C-corp E&P → automatic termination
view.s1362.term.revocation: Revocation: majority shareholder consent + Form 2553A statement
view.s1362.terminated_note: S election TERMINATED automatically. 5-year wait before re-election (IRS consent required to shorten). Apply for inadvertent termination relief under § 1362(f) if facts permit — requires showing termination was inadvertent + cured promptly + reasonable. PLR application typically required. Tax cost: C-corp double tax until cured.
view.s1366.basis.decrease: DECREASE basis: distributions + losses + sep stated losses + non-deductible
view.s1366.basis.guarantee: Guarantee of S-corp debt does NOT create debt basis (need direct loan)
view.s1366.basis.increase: INCREASE basis: ordinary income + sep stated income + tax-exempt int + cap contributions
view.s1366.basis.order: Order: increases first, then distributions, then losses
view.s1366.basis.restore_debt: Repaid debt basis: gain to extent basis restored if reduced
view.s1366.basis.restored: Suspended loss claimed when basis restored (additional contribution / income)
view.s1366.basis.stock_first: Stock basis used first for losses; then debt basis (from direct loans only)
view.s1366.basis.suspended: Loss in excess of basis SUSPENDED → carry forward indefinitely
view.s1366.char.charitable: Charitable contributions: subject to shareholder's AGI limits (60%, 30%, 20%)
view.s1366.char.dpad: § 199A QBI 20% deduction for active trade or business income
view.s1366.char.niit: § 1411 NIIT 3.8%: applies if shareholder is passive
view.s1366.char.qdiv: Qualified dividends keep qualified character + 60-day holding
view.s1366.char.s1231: § 1231 gain / loss: net at shareholder level → cap gain if net gain, ordinary if net loss
view.s1366.char.salt: SALT taxes on K-1: subject to $10K cap (BBA-PTET workaround)
view.s1366.char.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt interest: keeps exempt character (raises basis without inclusion)
view.s1366.h1.title: // § 1366 S-CORP PASS-THROUGH
view.s1366.hint.intro: Shareholder includes pro-rata share of S-corp income, loss, deduction, credit. Items keep CHARACTER — LTCG stays LTCG, qualified div stays qualified, § 1231 separately. Pass-through tracked daily — per-day allocation for mid-year ownership change or election. Loss limited to OUTSIDE BASIS (§ 1366(d)), then AT-RISK (§ 465), then PAL (§ 469). Carryforward indefinitely until basis restored. QBI § 199A 20% deduction available.
view.s1366.suspended_note: Suspended loss carries forward indefinitely until basis restored via additional capital contribution OR future S-corp income. Consider loan-from-shareholder (direct, not guarantee) to create debt basis and unlock loss.
view.s1368.aaa.add_income: Add: ordinary income + sep stated income + tax-exempt interest INCOME
view.s1368.aaa.can_be_negative: AAA can be NEGATIVE from losses (but distribution limited to current AAA)
view.s1368.aaa.distributions_last: Distributions subtracted LAST (reduce AAA, not below zero)
view.s1368.aaa.net_negative: Net negative AAA distributions skipped — go to E&P tier
view.s1368.aaa.s382_ats: Coordinate with § 382 AAA limitation upon ownership change
view.s1368.aaa.subtract_loss: Subtract: ordinary loss + sep stated losses + nondeductible expenses
view.s1368.aaa.tax_exempt_oaa: Tax-exempt interest goes to OAA (not AAA) — corrected by 2002 regs
view.s1368.bypass_note: AAA bypass election in effect: distribute E&P first to permanently eliminate C-corp E&P. Valuable cleanup when shareholders prefer dividend treatment now (e.g., DRD-eligible corp shareholder) over future deferral.
view.s1368.elect.aaa_cleanup: Permanent cleanup: use bypass election to eliminate accumulated C-corp E&P
view.s1368.elect.bypass: § 1368(e)(3) AAA bypass: distribute from E&P FIRST (use up old C-corp E&P)
view.s1368.elect.bypass_rationale: AAA bypass valuable if shareholder is C-corp with DRD vs individual w/ QDR
view.s1368.elect.close_books: § 1377(a)(2) close books: avoid blending pre/post-event allocation
view.s1368.elect.consent: Election requires consent of ALL affected shareholders (Form 1120-S box)
view.s1368.elect.qsst_esbt: QSST / ESBT trust beneficiary elections: affect distribution flow
view.s1368.h1.title: // § 1368 S-CORP DISTRIBUTIONS
view.s1368.h2.ordering: Standard ordering for S-corp w/ C-corp E&P
view.s1368.hint.intro: S-corp without C-corp E&P: distribution reduces basis → return of capital, then capital gain. S-corp with C-corp E&P: AAA first (tax-free up to basis), then E&P (taxable dividend), then OAA / basis. AAA = Accumulated Adjustments Account. PTI = Previously Taxed Income (pre-1983 carryover). OAA = Other Adjustments Account (tax-exempt interest). Schedule M-2 of Form 1120-S.
view.s1368.order.aaa: AAA: tax-free (reduces basis); excess over AAA → next tier
view.s1368.order.basis: Stock basis: tax-free (reduces basis to zero)
view.s1368.order.cg: Excess: CAPITAL GAIN (LTCG if > 1 yr holding)
view.s1368.order.ep: Accumulated E&P: TAXABLE DIVIDEND at qualified rate
view.s1368.order.modify: § 1368(e)(3) elections modify ordering at corporate level
view.s1368.order.no_ep: If S-corp has NO C-corp E&P: skip dividend tier — pure basis recovery
view.s1368.order.pti: PTI: tax-free (no basis effect — pre-1983 holdover)
view.s1374.card.subject: Subject to BIG
view.s1374.gotcha.aet: § 532 Accumulated Earnings Tax 20% (C-corp only) on undistributed earnings
view.s1374.gotcha.dpad: QBI § 199A may benefit from S vs C — analyze QBI limitations
view.s1374.gotcha.lifo: § 1363(d) LIFO recapture on C → S conversion (one-time pay over 4 yrs)
view.s1374.gotcha.passive: § 1375 passive investment income (PII) > 25% for 3 consecutive yrs → S-status revoked + 35% tax
view.s1374.gotcha.phc: § 541 Personal Holding Company 20% tax on undistributed personal holding income
view.s1374.h1.title: // § 1374 S-CORP BIG TAX
view.s1374.hint.intro: Former C-corp converting to S-corp: corporate-level 21% tax on gain accrued PRE-conversion when realized within 5-year recognition period. Limited to NUBIG (Net Unrealized Built-In Gain at conversion). BIG income flows to shareholders AFTER corp tax (double tax during 5-yr window). Reduces NUBIG dollar-for-dollar. Goal: hold appreciated assets ≥ 5 yrs post-conversion.
view.s1374.label.nubig_used: NUBIG already used in prior years ($)
view.s1374.plan.5_yr: Hold appreciated assets ≥ 5 yrs post-conversion → BIG period expires
view.s1374.plan.allocation: Allocate gain to assets WITHOUT built-in gain
view.s1374.plan.appraisal: Pre-conversion appraisal: low FMV reduces NUBIG
view.s1374.plan.charitable: Charitable contribution of appreciated property eliminates BIG
view.s1374.plan.installment: § 453 installment sale spreads BIG over years (still within window though)
view.s1374.plan.like_kind: § 1031 exchange defers BIG (real estate post-TCJA only)
view.s1374.plan.short_recog: CARES Act / Bush Tax Relief: 5-yr recognition period (down from 10-yr)
view.s1374.plan.ti_limit: BIG limited to corp-level taxable income (recognition deferred if no income)
view.s1377.ed.consent: CONSENT: ALL affected shareholders (departing + new + remaining) + corp
view.s1377.ed.flip_disadvantage: Disadvantage flips: useful for one party, harmful for other — consent reflects negotiation
view.s1377.ed.irrevocable: Once made for a year: irrevocable for that year
view.s1377.ed.partial: Can be made for specific change events, not all
view.s1377.ed.timing: Made on Form 1120-S for year of change
view.s1377.ed.use_case_gain: Use case: heavy gain in second half (new partner reaps; departing avoids tax)
view.s1377.ed.use_case_loss: Use case: heavy losses in first half (new partner takes hit; departing avoids)
view.s1377.election_note: Closing-books election reflects ACTUAL income pattern instead of arbitrary daily pro-rata. Valuable when income lumpy — e.g., legal practice w/ year-end fee receipts; construction contract billed at completion; SaaS w/ annual billing. Consent of all shareholders required — negotiation often results in side payments to compensate disadvantaged party.
view.s1377.h1.title: // § 1377 S-CORP ALLOC + PTTP
view.s1377.hint.intro: § 1377(a)(1) DEFAULT: pro-rata DAILY allocation when shareholder changes mid-year. § 1377(a)(2) Closing-of-Books Election: avoid pro-rata — allocate based on ACTUAL income in pre / post-change periods. Requires CONSENT of ALL affected shareholders + corp. Useful when income lumpy / heavy in one period. § 1377(b) PTTP (Post-Termination Transition Period): 1 year + 120 days after S election terminates to distribute AAA tax-free. § 1377(a)(2) v. § 1362(e)(3): different elections — term vs change in shareholder.
view.s1377.pttp.aaa_freeze: AAA balance FREEZES at termination — no further income additions
view.s1377.pttp.attributes_after: Post-PTTP distributions: ordinary dividend (subject to qualified div rules)
view.s1377.pttp.distributions: Distributions during PTTP treated as AAA / PTI / OAA — tax-free to extent
view.s1377.pttp.elect_out: § 1371(e)(2) elect out: revoke step-up to FMV (rarely used)
view.s1377.pttp.no_extension: Cannot extend PTTP beyond statutory window
view.s1377.pttp.purpose: Purpose: allow former S-corp shareholders to cash out without dividend tax
view.s1377.pttp.s1368_ordering: Standard § 1368 ordering: AAA → PTI → OAA → basis → cap gain
view.s1377.pttp.window: Window: 1 YEAR after termination + 120 days for late challenges
view.s1400z.comp.5_pct_finance: < 5% Nonqualified financial property (excludes working capital ≤ 31 months under 'safe harbor')
view.s1400z.comp.86_month: 31-month 'working capital safe harbor' for QOZ Business — written plan + schedule
view.s1400z.comp.90pct: Hold 90%+ assets in QOZ Property (semiannual test, average of 2 dates)
view.s1400z.comp.original_use: Original use in QOZ — OR substantial improvement (double basis within 30 months)
view.s1400z.comp.qoz_business: QOZ Business: 70% of tangible property in QOZ + 50% of gross income from QOZ + < 5% nonqualified financial property
view.s1400z.comp.qoz_property: QOZ Property: QOZ Business Property OR equity in QOZ Business OR partnership int
view.s1400z.comp.sin_business: Sin businesses excluded: golf, country clubs, massage parlors, hot tubs, sun tan, racetracks, gambling, liquor stores
view.s1400z.elig.180_day: 180-day window: starts at realization (for individual) or partnership year-end
view.s1400z.elig.capital: Capital gain only (short OR long term)
view.s1400z.elig.not_ordinary: Ordinary income gain NOT eligible (recapture, inventory, etc.)
view.s1400z.elig.not_unrealized: Unrealized gain NOT eligible — must be realized first
view.s1400z.elig.pass_through: Pass-through K-1 gain — partner / S-shareholder reinvests
view.s1400z.exit.10yr_step_up: 10-yr+ hold: full exclusion on QOF appreciation (not original deferred gain)
view.s1400z.exit.death_basis: Death of investor: § 1014 basis step-up may eliminate deferred gain
view.s1400z.exit.dec_2026: Dec 31 2026: forced inclusion event — pay deferred gain tax
view.s1400z.exit.gift: Gift to family: inclusion event (taxable to donor)
view.s1400z.exit.partial_disposition: Partial QOF interest disposition: pro-rata inclusion
view.s1400z.exit.qof_fail: QOF fails 90% test: monthly penalty $10K min (Section 6700-style)
view.s1400z.exit.qof_sale: Earlier QOF sale: inclusion at sale date
view.s1400z.exit.s1031_no: § 1031 within QOF not allowed (TCJA removed personal property)
view.s1400z.h1.title: // § 1400Z-2 OPPORTUNITY ZONES
view.s1400z.hint.intro: Tier 1 — Deferral: defer capital gain by investing in QOF within 180 days of realization → defer until earlier of QOF sale or Dec 31 2026. Tier 2 — Step-up: 5-yr hold → 10% basis step-up (15% if pre-2020 + 7-yr hold). Tier 3 — Exclusion: 10-yr hold → BASIS STEP-UP TO FMV → EXCLUDE APPRECIATION on QOF investment. QOF: 90%+ of assets in QOZ Property. Form 8996 (QOF) + Form 8997 (investor). Designation through 2028.
view.s1400z.tier3_note: Tier 3 unlocked: 10-yr hold provides BASIS STEP-UP TO FMV on QOF interest sale. Full appreciation excluded — including LTCG from QOF business operations. Combined with Tier 1 deferral + Tier 2 step-up, this is the largest tax shelter available.
view.s1402.excl.investment: Investment income: dividends, interest, capital gains (incl. trader gains)
view.s1402.excl.s_corp_dist: S-corp distributions to shareholder-employees (above reasonable comp)
view.s1402.excl.tts_trader: § 475(f) trader: TTS trading income NOT SE (cap gains, not personal services)
view.s1402.h1.title: // § 1402 SELF-EMPLOYMENT TAX
view.s1402.h2.exclusions: Income NOT subject to SE tax
view.s1402.hint.intro: 15.3% on net SE × 0.9235: 12.4% SS + 2.9% Medicare. SS wage base 2024: $168,600; Medicare uncapped. Additional 0.9% Medicare over $200k single / $250k MFJ. Half-SE-tax deduction § 164(f) above-the-line. W-2 wages from same employer REDUCE SS-base SE tax dollar-for-dollar. Form SE attached to 1040.
view.s1402.opt.qbi: QBI deduction § 199A still applies to SE income
view.s1402.opt.retirement: Solo 401(k) / SEP / SIMPLE max with QBI-friendly structure
view.s1402.opt.s_corp: S-corp election + reasonable comp: distributions escape SE tax
view.s1402.opt.spouse_split: Spouse partnership splitting: each gets own SS-base wage cap
view.s1411.excl.active_kone: Active S-corp distributions / K-1 if material participation
view.s1411.excl.section_988: § 988 forex ordinary if held as trader
view.s1411.h1.title: // § 1411 NIIT 3.8% SURTAX
view.s1411.hint.intro: 3.8% surtax on net investment income when MAGI > $200k single / $250k MFJ / $125k MFS. Tax base = LESSER of (1) net investment income OR (2) MAGI excess over threshold. § 475(f) MTM ordinary EXCLUDED (huge trader benefit). Active S-corp earnings EXCLUDED. Retirement distributions EXCLUDED. Reported on Form 8960. Not indexed for inflation.
view.s1411.plan.475f: § 475(f) MTM election if TTS-qualified — escape NIIT entirely on trading
view.s1411.plan.bunching: Bunch investment income into low-MAGI years
view.s1411.plan.material: Establish material participation in passive activities — recharacterize as active
view.s1411.plan.muni: Shift to municipal bonds — interest excluded
view.s1411.plan.qof: § 1400Z Opportunity Zone investment — defer + reduce gain
view.s1411.plan.roth: Roth conversions — distributions exempt forever after qualifying
view.s1445.b_note: Form 8288-B pending: IRS issues Withholding Certificate within 90 days; escrow held until determination. Reduce withholding to actual computed tax. Closing proceeds may be deposited in escrow while certificate pending.
view.s1445.h1.title: // § 1445 FIRPTA WITHHOLDING
view.s1445.hint.intro: Buyer (transferee) withholds 15% of amount realized on USRPI disposition by foreign person. 10% for personal residence ≤ $1M; 0% for personal use ≤ $300K (buyer affidavit required). Foreign seller claims credit on 1040NR / 1120-F. Reduced withholding certificate via Form 8288-B pre-closing. Penalties on buyer for failure to withhold. Forms 8288, 8288-A, 8288-B.
view.s1445.proc.8288b: Pre-closing Form 8288-B: reduce withholding to actual tax — IRS has 90 days to respond
view.s1445.proc.agent: Settlement agent / escrow agent often handles mechanical withholding
view.s1445.proc.closing: Buyer withholds at closing — deducts from sale proceeds delivered to seller
view.s1445.proc.escrow: Common practice: escrow withholding until 8288-B determination
view.s1445.proc.penalty: Buyer penalty: tax liability + interest if fails to withhold
view.s1445.proc.refund: Seller refund: file 1040NR (individual) or 1120-F (corp) for actual tax + claim withholding credit
view.s1445.proc.remit: Remit to IRS within 20 days of closing with Form 8288
view.s1445.proc.statement: Provide Form 8288-A copy to seller — needed for refund claim
view.s1446F.eci.character: Character: same as if assets directly sold (cap gain, ordinary, recapture)
view.s1446F.eci.computation: ECI gain = total gain × FMV of ECI assets / FMV of all assets
view.s1446F.eci.deemed: Foreign partner deemed to sell ECI assets pro-rata on sale of interest
view.s1446F.eci.ftc: Foreign FTC may apply if foreign country also taxes the gain
view.s1446F.eci.rev_91_32: Reverses Rev. Rul. 91-32 (only US assets treated as sourced US) — TCJA codified deemed sale
view.s1446F.eci.s897_combined: Coordinate with § 897 FIRPTA — overlapping for partnerships with US real property
view.s1446F.eci.us_rate: Taxed at regular US rates (max 37% individual / 21% corp)
view.s1446F.h1.title: // § 1446(f) PS INT. WITHHOLDING
view.s1446F.hint.intro: Transferee withholds 10% of amount realized on sale of US trade/biz partnership interest by foreign person (TCJA 2018). ECI portion deemed sold by foreign partner → may be subject to higher US tax. Exceptions: seller certifies non-foreign (Form W-8 + W-9), seller certifies no ECI portion, partnership certifies low %, PTP exception, withholding certificate. Forms 8804 + 8805 + 8288 series. Coordinate with § 1445 FIRPTA (real estate).
view.s1446F.ptp.broker: Broker (not buyer) handles withholding on sales of PTP units
view.s1446F.ptp.cure: No correction filed if broker withholds correctly per Notice 2018-29
view.s1446F.ptp.distributions: PTP distributions to foreign partners: § 1446(a) withholding (37% indiv / 21% corp)
view.s1446F.ptp.exemption: If PTP < 10% ECI: no § 1446(f) withholding on sales
view.s1446F.ptp.major_ptps: Major PTPs: Energy Transfer Partners, Enterprise Products, MPLX, etc.
view.s1446F.ptp.qualified: 'Qualified notice' required: PTP certifies % ECI to broker
view.s1446F.ptp.report: Form 1042-S issued by broker for withholding
view.s1446F.refund_note: Withholding exceeds actual tax liability. File for refund via Form 1040NR / 1120-F. Alternative: pre-closing withholding certificate (similar to § 1445 Form 8288-B) reduces withholding to actual tax. IRS has 90 days to respond — typically escrow held until issued.
view.s1446F.scope.10pct: Default rate: 10% of TOTAL amount realized
view.s1446F.scope.eci_only: Reduced rate available if seller certifies LIMITED ECI exposure
view.s1446F.scope.ecit: Partnership conducts US trade or business (creates ECI on portion)
view.s1446F.scope.foreign_seller: SELLER is foreign person (NRA, foreign corp, foreign partnership)
view.s1446F.scope.partnership_secondary: Partnership becomes secondarily liable if transferee fails
view.s1446F.scope.penalty: Penalty: 100% of withholding amount + interest if failure
view.s1446F.scope.remit: Remit to IRS within 20 days of payment
view.s1446F.scope.transferee: TRANSFEREE (buyer of partnership interest) must withhold
view.s152.h1.title: // § 152 DEPENDENT QUALIFICATION
view.s152.hint.intro: Two categories: (1) Qualifying Child (QC) — relationship + age (< 19 or < 24 student or any age disabled) + residency > ½ yr + did not provide > ½ own support, (2) Qualifying Relative (QR) — relationship OR household member full year + gross income < $5,050 (2024) + support > ½. Multiple Support Agreement (Form 2120) if no one provides > 50%.
view.s152.qr.aunts_uncles: Aunt, uncle, niece, nephew (by blood ONLY, not in-law)
view.s152.qr.member: Any unrelated person who lives with you ENTIRE year
view.s152.qr.not_violation: Relationship not violation of local law (e.g., not bigamy)
view.s152.tb.agreement: Multiple support agreement (Form 2120): > 10% support each, sign-off
view.s152.tb.divorced: Custodial parent default unless Form 8332 release signed
view.s152.tb.highest_agi: Equal time — parent with higher AGI
view.s152.tb.most_time: If both parents — parent with MORE residency time
view.s152.tb.no_parents: No parent claims — highest AGI taxpayer can claim
view.s162a1.doc.board_minutes: Board / shareholder minutes documenting comp decision + rationale
view.s162a1.doc.bonus_clawback: Bonus clawback provisions strengthen reasonable comp argument
view.s162a1.doc.compensation_study: Annual compensation study: benchmark to similar companies / positions
view.s162a1.doc.contemporaneous: Decisions contemporaneous with payment (not after-the-fact rationalization)
view.s162a1.doc.employment_agreement: Written employment agreement with specific salary / bonus provisions
view.s162a1.doc.performance_metrics: Performance metrics + bonus formulas in writing
view.s162a1.doc.s_corp_compensation_stud: RCReports + Equilar + BLS data: industry benchmarks
view.s162a1.doc.salary_history: Maintain salary history showing pattern over years
view.s162a1.h1.title: // § 162(a)(1) REASONABLE COMP
view.s162a1.hint.intro: Deductible compensation must be REASONABLE in amount for services actually rendered. Excess: characterized as DISGUISED DIVIDEND (C-corp) → NOT DEDUCTIBLE distribution. Tests: Mayson Manufacturing 5-factor + Independent Investor Test (Exacto Spring) + Multi-Factor (most circuits). Disputes: closely-held corps, family businesses. S-corp REVERSE problem: owner-employees UNDERPAY salary to avoid SE tax → IRS recharacterizes distributions as wages.
view.s162a1.iit.combined: Court often applies BOTH tests in combination
view.s162a1.iit.deficient: If hypothetical investor would be dissatisfied → comp EXCESSIVE
view.s162a1.iit.industry: Compare to: industry benchmark return on capital
view.s162a1.iit.minority: Other circuits still use 5-factor (Tax Court general approach)
view.s162a1.iit.return: Look at: return on equity / assets after deducting compensation
view.s162a1.iit.satisfied: If hypothetical investor would be content → comp REASONABLE
view.s162a1.iit.test: Test: would an independent investor in the corp be content with the return after compensation?
view.s162a1.mayson.character: Character + condition of company: size, complexity, financial position
view.s162a1.mayson.conflict_interest: Conflict of interest: any inherent bias (owner-employee, family)
view.s162a1.mayson.external: External comparisons: similar positions in similar companies
view.s162a1.mayson.internal_consistency: Internal consistency: comp pattern over time + similar employees
view.s162a1.mayson.role: Employee's role: position, hours worked, duties
view.s162a1.scr.cases: Watson v. Comm'r (2010): $24K salary on $371K K-1 distribution rejected — IRS adjusted to $90K
view.s162a1.scr.factors: Factors: training, experience, duties, time devoted, comparable positions, employee dividend history
view.s162a1.scr.flow_through: Recharacterization triggers: payroll taxes + Form 941 + W-2 amendments
view.s162a1.scr.irs: IRS recharacterizes distribution as wages → adds back SE / FICA + penalties
view.s162a1.scr.payroll_taxes: Reasonable comp study: PRO documents salary determination
view.s162a1.scr.problem: S-corp owners pay themselves LOW salary + take large distributions to avoid SE/FICA
view.s162a1.scr.s262: IRS Fact Sheet 2008-25 + Notice 2018-2: documentation expectations
view.s162a1.scr.test: Test: REASONABLE comp for services rendered
view.s162a1.unreasonable_note: Compensation NOT reasonable: C-corp deduction disallowed for excess (DOUBLE TAX as dividend); S-corp reclassification triggers SE tax + payroll taxes + penalties. Obtain compensation study (RCReports, Equilar, BLS) + document board decision + maintain contemporaneous records.
view.s162c.cases.alcan: Alcan Aluminium (1998): Canadian tax law factor in foreign legality
view.s162c.cases.commissioner: Commissioner v. Tellier (1966): legal fees in defense of business deductible (NOT § 162(c))
view.s162c.cases.dichter: Dichter v. Commissioner: legitimate vs disguised commissions
view.s162c.cases.goldman_1mdb: Goldman Sachs / 1MDB $3.3B settlement (2020): partial restitution / penalty allocation
view.s162c.cases.hca: HCA $1.7B Medicare fraud settlement: anti-kickback dimension
view.s162c.cases.siemens: Siemens FCPA $800M (2008): largest FCPA settlement — all nondeductible
view.s162c.cases.smith_wessel: Smith v. Commissioner: kickback definition fact-intensive
view.s162c.fcpa.basic: FCPA prohibits bribery of foreign officials to obtain / retain business
view.s162c.fcpa.cdr: Books + records provisions require accurate financial reporting
view.s162c.fcpa.facilitating: Facilitating ('grease') payments: limited exception under FCPA, STILL NONDEDUCTIBLE under § 162(c)
view.s162c.fcpa.payor: Applies to: US persons + entities + foreign issuers (15 U.S.C. § 78dd-1)
view.s162c.fcpa.successor_liability: Successor liability: acquiring corp may inherit FCPA exposure
view.s162c.fcpa.uk_bribery: UK Bribery Act 2010: more strict (no facilitating payment exception)
view.s162c.h1.title: // § 162(c) ILLEGAL PAYMENTS
view.s162c.hint.intro: NO DEDUCTION for amounts constituting illegal bribes, kickbacks, other illegal payments. § 162(c)(1): payments to government officials — federal, state, local, FOREIGN. § 162(c)(2): kickbacks to non-government persons / entities. § 162(c)(3): kickbacks tied to Medicare / Medicaid fraud. FCPA (Foreign Corrupt Practices Act) violations: nondeductible. Burden: taxpayer must show payment NOT illegal. Conviction: not required — IRS can disallow based on facts + law analysis.
view.s162c.illegal_note: Payment NONDEDUCTIBLE under § 162(c). Even without DOJ prosecution / conviction, IRS may disallow based on legal substance. FCPA + Anti-Kickback Statute violations particularly high risk. Consider self-disclosure to DOJ for cooperation credit + reduce penalty exposure. Sec/SEC + DOJ + IRS exchange information.
view.s162c.med.compliance_program: Compliance program: Office of Inspector General (OIG) recommendations
view.s162c.med.consulting_fees: 'Consulting fees' disguised as referral compensation: scrutinized
view.s162c.med.intent: Intent: anything of value INTENDED to induce referrals
view.s162c.med.qui_tam: Qui tam (False Claims Act): whistleblower 15-30% reward share
view.s162c.med.safe_harbors: Safe harbors: bona fide employee, GPO (Group Purchasing), space rental, equipment rental
view.s162c.sub.1: § 162(c)(1): payments to government officials (any govt) — NEVER deductible
view.s162c.sub.2_burden: Burden: taxpayer must prove payment is NOT illegal
view.s162c.sub.3_safe_harbor: Anti-kickback statute safe harbors: legitimate payment structures
view.s162c.sub.legal_referrals: Legal referrals (with Stark Law compliance): deductible
view.s162c.sub.proof: No conviction required — IRS analyzes legal substance
view.s162f.f.boxes: Form has separate boxes: (a) penalty, (b) restitution, (c) compliance, (d) other
view.s162f.f.consistent: Taxpayer must REPORT CONSISTENTLY with 1098-F amounts
view.s162f.f.copy_to_taxpayer: Copy to taxpayer; copy to IRS
view.s162f.f.dispute: Dispute: file amended 1098-F or claim different on 1040 with explanation
view.s162f.f.exception: Exception: settlement payments to private plaintiffs not subject to 1098-F
view.s162f.f.gov_files: Government MUST file Form 1098-F if total ≥ $50K
view.s162f.f.no_form_no_deduction: No 1098-F received: presumption is no restitution / compliance identified
view.s162f.f.timing: Filed in year payment made (cash basis for both parties)
view.s162f.h1.title: // § 162(f) FINES + PENALTIES
view.s162f.hint.intro: NO DEDUCTION for fines / similar penalties paid to government / governmental entity. TCJA 2017 expanded: all government, even foreign. Exceptions: (1) IDENTIFIED RESTITUTION (to victim), (2) IDENTIFIED COMPLIANCE costs. Settlement language CRITICAL: must specifically IDENTIFY which portion is restitution vs penalty. § 6050X reporting: government MUST report payments to IRS via Form 1098-F. § 162(g): 2/3 of antitrust treble damages also nondeductible.
view.s162f.id.allocation: Cannot ALLOCATE after the fact — must be PRE-SET in settlement document
view.s162f.id.allocation_rare: Specific dollar amount + clear restitution / compliance label required
view.s162f.id.amount: Amount specifically identified (not generic / pro-rata after the fact)
view.s162f.id.preparer_burden: Document settlement carefully — IRS examiners challenge allocations
view.s162f.id.reasonable_basis: 'Reasonable basis to expect' amounts identified would be paid
view.s162f.id.regulation: Reg § 1.162-21 (2021 final regs) clarifies identification standard
view.s162f.id.specific: Settlement / court order must SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFY portion as restitution / compliance
view.s162f.no_form_note: No Form 1098-F received: IRS presumes ALL payment to government is nondeductible. Critical pre-settlement step: NEGOTIATE settlement language identifying specific dollar amounts as restitution / compliance. Post-settlement allocation usually too late. Consult tax counsel during settlement negotiations.
view.s162f.rel.c: § 162(c): illegal payments / kickbacks NONDEDUCTIBLE (foreign bribes too)
view.s162f.rel.k: § 162(k): healthcare premium for retirees subject to limits
view.s162f.rel.m: § 162(m): $1M deduction limit on public co exec comp
view.s162f.rel.s: Smith / Jenkins / Westmoreland cases established narrow restitution interpretation
view.s162f.scope.civil: Civil penalties (DOJ, EPA, SEC, CFTC, FTC, state attorneys general)
view.s162f.scope.environmental: Environmental penalties (EPA Clean Air, Clean Water, RCRA)
view.s162f.scope.fine: Fines + penalties paid to government for violation of law
view.s162f.scope.opioid: Opioid settlements (recent wave): mostly NONDEDUCTIBLE under § 162(f)
view.s162l.h1.title: // § 162(l) SE HEALTH INSURANCE
view.s162l.hint.intro: Above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) for medical / dental / LTC premiums paid by self-employed, partners, and > 2% S-corp shareholders. Reduces AGI (not just Schedule A). LTC limited by age table. S-corp: premiums must be paid by corp + included in W-2 Box 1 wages + reported in Box 14 (not Box 3/5). DISALLOWED if eligible for spouse's subsidized employer plan.
view.s162l.s_corp.payor: Premiums must be paid by S-corp (not personally)
view.s162l.s_corp.reasonable_comp: Must have reasonable comp before § 162(l) deduction allowed
view.s162l.s_corp.shareholder: Owner deducts on personal return Schedule 1 line 17
view.s162l.s_corp.w2: Must be added to W-2 Box 1 (taxable income) + Box 14 (informational)
view.s162l.warning.spouse_plan: DISALLOWED: § 162(l)(2)(B) blocks the deduction in any month you're eligible for a subsidized employer plan via spouse. Pay premiums + take Schedule A 7.5% AGI floor instead.
view.s162m.h1.title: // § 162(m) $1M EXEC COMP CAP
view.s162m.hint.intro: Public C-corps disallowed deduction for comp > $1M paid to covered employees: CEO, CFO, three highest-paid officers + any covered employee since 2017 (sticky list — once covered, always covered). TCJA 2017 eliminated performance-based exception. ARPA 2021 expanded to top 5 highest-paid (not just executives) starting 2027.
view.s162m.label.name: Name / role
view.s162m.label.title: Title
view.s162m.plan.deferred: Defer comp until post-separation year if person no longer in scope
view.s162m.plan.foreign_subs: Foreign sub of public US co: § 162(m) applies if subject to Securities Act reporting
view.s162m.plan.ipo: IPO transition relief: 3 yr post-IPO has reduced rules
view.s162m.plan.q1: SEC Form Summary Comp Table is § 162(m) starting point
view.s162m.plan.section_280g: Coordinate with § 280G golden parachute disallowance + 20% excise
view.s162m.th.name: Name
view.s162m.th.title: Title
view.s163j.ati.post_2022: Post-2022: do NOT add back D + A (depreciation + amortization stays out of ATI)
view.s163j.elect_note: Irrevocable elect-out triggers ADS depreciation requirement (40-yr nonres real, 30-yr res real, longer farming class lives). Permanent — cannot reverse.
view.s163j.exempt.consumer: Floor-plan financing (auto, boat, RV dealers): always fully deductible
view.s163j.exempt.farming: Farming business: irrevocable elect-out at cost of ADS depreciation
view.s163j.exempt.real: Real property trade: irrevocable elect-out at cost of ADS depreciation (40-yr nonres)
view.s163j.exempt.relative: Partner BIE limit applied at partnership level (not partner level) — EBIE allocation
view.s163j.exempt.s_corp: S-corp limit applied at corporate level (no partner-style EBIE)
view.s163j.exempt.tax_shelter: Tax shelter NEVER qualifies regardless of size
view.s163j.exempt.utility: Electing utility business: exempt for regulated rate base
view.s163j.h1.title: // § 163(j) BUSINESS INTEREST LIMIT
view.s163j.hint.intro: BIE deduction ≤ business interest INCOME + 30% × ATI + floor-plan financing. Pre-2022: ATI based on EBITDA (more generous). Post-2022: ATI based on EBIT (TCJA stricter — depreciation + amortization excluded). Small biz exception: ≤ $30M avg gross receipts (3-yr). Real property + farming trades may irrevocably elect out (cost: ADS depreciation). Disallowed interest carries forward indefinitely (corp + partnership pass-through with EBIE rules).
view.s164.h1.title: // § 164 TAXES DEDUCTION + SALT CAP
view.s164.hint.intro: $10K SALT cap (2018-2025, MFS $5K) on state/local income/sales + property tax. Business + rental property tax NOT capped. PTET workaround in 36 states.
view.s165.h1.title: // § 165 LOSSES
view.s165.hint.intro: 4 categories: trade/business, profit transaction, personal casualty (federally declared disasters only post-TCJA), wagering. Worthless securities + Ponzi safe harbor.
view.s165c3.h1.title: // § 165(c)(3) CASUALTY LOSS
view.s165c3.hint.intro: TCJA 2018-2025: ONLY federally-declared disaster casualty losses allowed for non-business. Loss = lesser of (1) basis - insurance OR (2) decline in FMV. Reduce by (a) $100 per casualty + (b) 10% of AGI. § 165(i) election: claim loss in PRIOR year for faster refund (must file by amended return or original return within statute). Qualified disaster losses (special): $500 floor + no 10% AGI test.
view.s165c3.qe.hurricane: Hurricane, tornado, flood, fire, earthquake (federally declared)
view.s165c3.qe.theft: Theft (post-TCJA: ONLY if federally declared disaster context)
view.s165c3.warning.tcja: TCJA 2018-2025: only federally-declared disaster casualty losses allowed for non-business property. Personal loss otherwise NOT deductible.
view.s165d.h1.title: // § 165(d) GAMBLING LOSSES
view.s165d.hint.intro: Gambling LOSSES deductible ONLY to extent of GAMBLING WINNINGS. Excess losses NOT deductible / no carry-forward. Amateurs: winnings → Schedule 1 income; losses → Schedule A itemized (only if itemizing). Professionals: Schedule C trade / business — gambling expenses + losses limited to winnings under TCJA 2017. Mayo v. Comm'r (2018) — pre-TCJA expenses + losses cap to winnings questioned; TCJA codified strict limit. Form W-2G: payer reports winnings ≥ $600/$1,200/$5,000 thresholds.
view.s165d.no_itemize_note: Standard deduction beats itemizing — gambling losses DISALLOWED (cannot reduce winnings). Full winnings taxable. Common trap: amateur gambler with high standard deduction loses ALL benefit of losses. Consider professional status if activity is consistent + profit-motive demonstrable (5 Groetzinger factors).
view.s165d.rec.audit_risk: Audit risk: high if winnings + losses don't square; IRS cross-checks W-2Gs
view.s165d.rec.casino_card: Casino loyalty card statements (helpful but not authoritative)
view.s165d.rec.cohan: Cohan rule: courts may estimate if records inadequate (but disfavored)
view.s165d.rec.diary: Contemporaneous diary / log: date, location, type, amount, persons present
view.s165d.rec.receipts: Receipts: hotel, travel, parking, food (professional only)
view.s165d.rec.session_log: Session-based log preferred over per-bet detail
view.s165d.sess.crypto: Crypto gambling: each bet separate (depending on platform)
view.s165d.sess.documentation: Documentation: time, date, location, amount, type, persons present (Rev. Proc. 77-29)
view.s165d.sess.formula: Track buy-in + cash out per session
view.s165d.sess.poker: Poker / table games: per-table or per-tournament session
view.s165d.sess.purpose: Net within 'session' — discrete period (single day at casino)
view.s165d.sess.report: Report only NET WIN per session (vs gross-up of every spin)
view.s165d.sess.sports: Sports betting: per-bet basis (no session netting)
view.s165d.w2g.cap: Backup withholding 24% if no TIN provided (vs default 24%)
view.s165d.w2g.copy_irs: Casino files copy with IRS — match to your return
view.s165d.w2g.poker_tourn: Poker tournament: ≥ $5,000 reduced by entry fee (withholding 24% if > $5K)
view.s165g.empty: No worthless / terminated items for this year.
view.s165g.h1.title: // § 165(g) + § 1234A WORTHLESS / TERMINATED
view.s165g.hint.intro: § 165(g): security becomes worthless in year → capital loss treated as sold for $0 on Dec 31. Hold period extended to year-end: > 1 yr held = LT. § 165(g)(3) affiliate exception: parent owning ≥ 80% takes ORDINARY loss. § 1234A: terminated rights, cancelled options, expired forward contracts → capital loss on TERMINATION date, preserving holding period.
view.s165g.proof.documentation: Document: news, SEC filings, broker confirmation, expert opinion
view.s165g.proof.identifiable_event: 'Identifiable event' doctrine — bankruptcy filing OR cessation of trading
view.s165g.proof.no_liability: Delisting alone insufficient — need NO LIABILITY value remaining
view.s165g.proof.refund_claim: If wrong year identified: § 6511(d)(1) extends SOL to 7 yrs
view.s168.h1.title: // § 168 MACRS DEPRECIATION SCHEDULE
view.s168.hint.intro: Standard MACRS recovery periods: 3-yr (tools), 5-yr (computers, autos), 7-yr (office furniture), 15-yr (QIP, land improvements), 20-yr (farm buildings), 27.5-yr (residential rental, SL), 39-yr (non-residential real, SL). Half-year convention for most personal property; mid-quarter if > 40% Q4; mid-month for real property. QIP fixed by CARES Act 2020.
view.s168g.el.amt_minimization: Minimize AMT — straight-line method preferred for AMT
view.s168g.el.class_by_class: Election made class-by-class on Form 4562 line 20
view.s168g.el.foreign_tax_credit: Maximize foreign tax credit using slower domestic depreciation
view.s168g.el.future_high_rate: Defer deduction to future higher-rate year
view.s168g.el.no_bonus: No bonus depreciation on ADS property
view.s168g.h1.title: // § 168(g) ALTERNATIVE DEPRECIATION SYSTEM
view.s168g.hint.intro: Longer recovery periods + straight-line method. Required for: tax-exempt use property, listed property with ≤ 50% business use, certain imported property, § 263A(d)(3) post-2025 farming, BEAT property. ELECTION allowed for any class. No bonus depreciation on ADS property. Trade-off: ADS uses slower recovery but qualifies QIP for bonus retroactively (CARES Act fix).
view.s168g.req.imported: Imported from foreign trade restriction country
view.s168g.req.passive_pal: § 469 passive activity property in certain cases
view.s168g.req.tax_exempt: Tax-exempt use property (leased to govt / nonprofit / foreign person)
view.s168k.h1.title: // § 168(k) BONUS DEPRECIATION
view.s168k.hint.intro: TCJA phase-down: 100% (2017-22) → 80% (2023) → 60% (2024) → 40% (2025) → 20% (2026) → 0% (2027+). Property must be tangible, MACRS < 20 yr recovery, placed in service in the calendar year. Qualified Improvement Property (QIP) now eligible after CARES Act fix. Election OUT made class-by-class on timely return. Stacks under § 179 (which goes first).
view.s170.h1.title: // § 170 CHARITABLE CONTRIBUTIONS
view.s170.hint.intro: 60% AGI cash to public (2018-2025) / 30% cash to PF / 30% LTCG to public / 20% LTCG to PF. 5-year carryover. Substantiation $250+ requires written acknowledgment.
view.s172.cares.2018_2020: CARES Act 2020: TY 2018-2020 NOLs → 5-yr carryback + temporary 100% offset
view.s172.cares.382: § 382: ownership change ≥ 50% in 3-yr period limits NOL usage
view.s172.cares.farm_5yr: Farming NOLs: still 2-yr carryback option preserved
view.s172.cares.post_2020: 2021+: Back to TCJA rules (no carryback, 80% limit)
view.s172.cares.tcja: TCJA 2017: post-2017 NOLs no carryback, indefinite forward, 80% cap
view.s172.ebl.body: Effective 2018 (originally TCJA, was suspended 2018-2020 then reactivated). Non-corp taxpayers: aggregate business loss exceeding $305k single / $610k MFJ (2024) is DISALLOWED. Excess becomes NOL carryforward. Inflation Reduction Act 2022 extended § 461(l) through 2028.
view.s172.h1.title: // § 172 NET OPERATING LOSS CARRYFORWARD
view.s172.hint.intro: Pre-2018 NOLs: 2-yr carryback + 20-yr forward, 100% offset. Post-2017 NOLs: NO carryback (CARES 5-yr was 2018-2020 special), INDEFINITE forward, limited to 80% of taxable income. § 461(l) Excess Business Loss limit layered on top — $305k single / $610k MFJ (2024). Disallowed amount becomes NOL.
view.s172.plan.amt_nol: AMT NOL has separate tracking — not unified with regular NOL
view.s172.plan.at_risk: § 465 at-risk + § 469 passive activity layered on top of EBL + NOL
view.s172.plan.election: Pre-2018 carryback waiver election irrevocable — choose wisely
view.s172.plan.harvest: Realize gains in NOL year to use up loss carryforward
view.s172.plan.roth_recharacterize: Use NOL year to do large Roth conversion (absorbed at 0%)
view.s172.plan.s_corp_basis: S-corp basis must be tracked separately — limits flow-through loss usage
view.s174.h1.title: // § 174 R&D CAPITALIZATION
view.s174.hint.intro: TCJA 2017 forced mandatory capitalization of R&D effective tax years after 12/31/2021. US-based R&D: 5-year SL (60 months); foreign: 15-year SL (180 months). Mid-year convention: year-1 deduction is only 10% of US QREs (half of 1/5). Huge working-capital hit for tech / pharma / startups. § 41 R&D Credit unaffected. Repeal efforts ongoing.
view.s174.qual.contractors: Contractor R&D (65% allowed for credit; 100% capitalized)
view.s174.qual.patents: Patent attorney fees (acquisition costs, NOT defense)
view.s174.qual.software: In-house software development costs (Rev. Proc. 2000-50 prior, now § 174)
view.s174.qual.supplies: Supplies consumed in R&D (chemicals, prototypes, sensors)
view.s174.qual.wages: W-2 wages of researchers + supporting employees
view.s174.trans.cash_impact: Industry impact: many startups forced into book/tax NOLs
view.s174.trans.first_year: First mandatory year: TY beginning after 12/31/2021
view.s174.trans.method_change: Automatic method change (DCN 265) — no IRS consent
view.s174.trans.repeal_efforts: Multiple bipartisan repeal bills (Tax Relief for American Families 2024)
view.s174.trans.retroactive: Likely retroactive if repealed — file protective extension
view.s179d.combo.179: § 179 Expensing: separate from § 179D, can combine but reduces basis
view.s179d.combo.45L: § 45L New Energy Efficient Home: different — for residential / dwelling units
view.s179d.combo.6418: § 6418 Transferability: § 179D NOT in transferable credit list (deduction, not credit)
view.s179d.combo.bonus: § 168(k) Bonus depreciation: still allowed on basis after § 179D
view.s179d.combo.itc: § 48 ITC: separate solar investment tax credit overlap allowed
view.s179d.combo.state: State + local utility energy rebates often stack
view.s179d.designer.body: For government + non-profit buildings, building owner CANNOT claim § 179D (no tax liability). Owner can ALLOCATE deduction to qualifying designer (architect, engineer, contractor). Allocation by written certification. Designer treats as ordinary income recovery (basis reduction). One designer only per project; allocation does not affect cost basis to owner.
view.s179d.h1.title: // § 179D COMMERCIAL BUILDING ENERGY
view.s179d.hint.intro: IRA 2022 redesigned: $0.50-$5.65/sqft (2024) based on efficiency vs ASHRAE 90.1. Base $0.50/sqft + per-percentage-point lift over 25% ($0.02/sqft × pp). Prevailing wage + apprenticeship 5× multiplier. Allocation possible to designer (architect / engineer) for govt / non-profit buildings. Form 7205.
view.s179d.q.commercial: Commercial buildings (4+ floors); 4+ story mixed use
view.s179d.q.envelope: Building envelope: insulation, windows, doors, roof
view.s179d.q.govt: Government buildings (federal, state, local) — allocated to designer
view.s179d.q.tax_exempt: Non-profit / tax-exempt buildings (since IRA 2022) — allocated to designer
view.s179d.q.tribal: Tribal government / Alaska Native corp / Indian housing authority
view.s195.h1.title: // § 195 START-UP COST AMORTIZATION
view.s195.hint.intro: $5,000 immediate deduction in year of business opening + remainder amortized over 180 months (15 years). Phase-out: $5k cap reduces $1-for-$1 starting at $50k total expenses; gone at $55k. § 248 follows same rule for organizational costs (legal fees to form C-corp / partnership).
view.s195.nonqual.investigating: Investigating but NOT starting (only if business not pursued)
view.s195.qual.legal: Legal / accounting fees BEFORE opening (not § 248 org)
view.s195.qual.travel: Travel + consulting fees to secure suppliers, customers
view.s195.warning.phaseout: Total start-up costs exceed $50,000 phase-out threshold. Immediate deduction reduced $1-for-$1. Above $55,000 the entire $5k cap is gone — all costs go to 180-month amortization.
view.s197.h1.title: // § 197 INTANGIBLE AMORTIZATION
view.s197.hint.intro: Straight-line 15-year amortization on purchased intangibles. Covers goodwill, going concern, workforce in place, customer / supplier list, covenants not to compete, franchise / trademark / trade name, purchased patents / copyrights / software, license / permit / certificate. Self-created goodwill NOT subject to § 197 — capitalize as zero basis. 180-month line, mid-month convention.
view.s199a.h1.title: // § 199A QBI DEDUCTION
view.s199a.hint.intro: Up to 20% deduction on QBI from pass-through entities. 2024 thresholds: $241,950 single / $483,900 MFJ. SSTB completely phased out above upper threshold. Sunsets Dec 31, 2025.
view.s2010c.h1.title: // § 2010(c) DSUE PORTABILITY
view.s2010c.hint.intro: Surviving spouse claims Deceased Spousal Unused Exemption (DSUE). Election deadline: 9 months from death (15 with auto extension). Rev. Proc. 2022-32: simplified relief if not required to file — up to 5 years late. Without election, deceased spouse's unused exemption is FORFEITED. Combined exemption up to ~$27M (2024) without trust planning.
view.s2010c.note.simplified: Past 15-mo deadline but within 5 yrs — file under Rev. Proc. 2022-32 simplified relief. Mark Form 706 with 'FILED PURSUANT TO REV. PROC. 2022-32' at top.
view.s2010c.warn.assumed_below: Assumed estate too small to need 706 → DSUE forfeited
view.s2010c.warn.audit_open: Survivor's estate can re-open deceased's return to challenge DSUE valuation
view.s2010c.warn.gst_separate: GST exemption is NEVER portable — separate planning needed
view.s2010c.warn.last_remarriage: 'Last DSUE rule' — remarriage + death of new spouse without 706 → original DSUE lost
view.s2010c.warn.late_deadline: 9-month deadline missed → use Rev. Proc. 2022-32 simplified relief (5 yr)
view.s2010c.warn.no_inflation: DSUE FROZEN — survivor exemption indexes, deceased's does not
view.s2056.h1.title: // § 2056 MARITAL DEDUCTION + QTIP
view.s2056.hint.intro: Unlimited marital deduction for property passing to US-citizen surviving spouse. Non-citizen spouse: $185,000 (2024) annual exclusion + QDOT trust required for marital deduction. QTIP § 2056(b)(7): qualifying income-interest for life. First spouse uses exemption; second spouse gets income, NOT principal; remainder goes per first spouse's wishes. Common in blended families.
view.s2056.qdot.distribution_tax: Distributions of principal trigger DEFERRED estate tax at first decedent's rate
view.s2056.qdot.formula: Marital deduction reduces estate to LOWER OF actual transfer or QDOT amount
view.s2056.qdot.hardship: Income distributions + § 2056A hardship distributions exempt from estate tax
view.s2056.qdot.naturalization: Surviving spouse naturalizes → QDOT requirement ends
view.s2056.qdot.security: Trust assets > $2M: security bond or escrow OR > 35% in US real property
view.s2056.qdot.us_trustee: At least one US-citizen / US-corp trustee required
view.s2056.qtip.election: Executor must affirmatively elect QTIP on Form 706 within 9 mo + 6 mo extension
view.s2056.qtip.income_interest: Surviving spouse must receive ALL income annually for life
view.s2056.qtip.income_required: Income must be distributed at LEAST annually
view.s2056.qtip.no_gst: QTIP DOES NOT use GST exemption — separate election needed
view.s2056.qtip.no_others: No other person may receive income or principal during spouse's life
view.s2056.qtip.partial: Partial election allowed (only portion of trust qualifies)
view.s2056.qtip.reverse: Reverse QTIP election for GST: partial elect on portion for QTIP, reverse on rest
view.s2056.qtip.spouse_estate: QTIP property INCLUDED in surviving spouse's estate at death (§ 2044)
view.s2056.warning.non_citizen: Non-citizen spouse: marital deduction DENIED on outright transfers. Use QDOT trust. $185k (2024) annual exclusion for inter-spouse gifts during life; death-time transfers ONLY through QDOT.
view.s213.h1.title: // § 213 MEDICAL EXPENSE DEDUCTION
view.s213.hint.intro: Itemize only. Deduct medical / dental expenses > 7.5% of AGI. Spouse + dependent expenses count. LTC services + LTC premiums (age-limited) qualify. Capital expenditures for medical necessity (wheelchair ramp, lift, custom kitchen) also count. NOT deductible: cosmetic, gym memberships, vitamins, HSA-paid amounts (no double-dip), employer-reimbursed.
view.s213.qual.capital: Capital expenditures (wheelchair ramp, stair lift, modified vehicle) — reduce by increase in property value
view.s213.qual.doctors: Doctors, dentists, surgeons, chiropractors, psychiatrists, optometrists, podiatrists
view.s213.qual.special_school: Special education for learning disabilities (medical necessity)
view.s219.ap.401k: Eligible to participate in employer 401(k) / 403(b) / 457(b) — even if no contributions made
view.s219.ap.contributions: Received employer contributions during year (SEP, SIMPLE)
view.s219.ap.exclusions: NOT: § 457(b) gov't only (after 2007), pure deferred comp, 401(k) you joined late in year
view.s219.ap.federal: Federal Civil Service Retirement System / FERS
view.s219.dl.april_15: April 15 (or tax day) of following year for prior-year contribution
view.s219.dl.excess_removal: Excess contribution removal by Oct 15 (extended) avoids 6% penalty
view.s219.dl.no_extension: Extension to file does NOT extend contribution deadline
view.s219.dl.recharacterization: Roth conversion CANNOT be recharacterized (TCJA repeal)
view.s219.h1.title: // § 219 IRA DEDUCTION
view.s219.hint.intro: 2024: $7,000 base ($8,000 if 50+). Phase-out if active participant in employer plan: Single: $77k-$87k. MFJ (both active): $123k-$143k. MFJ (spouse active): $230k-$240k. No income limit if neither spouse covered by employer plan. Spousal IRA: working spouse covers non-working with own earned income. Roth (§ 408A) has different MAGI limits + no deduction.
view.s219.nd.50_penalty: Form 8606 failure to file: $50 penalty per year (Form 8606 standalone if no return)
view.s219.nd.cost_basis: Future Roth conversion: pro-rata between basis + earnings
view.s219.nd.form_8606: File Form 8606 every year to track basis (lifetime!)
view.s219.nd.pro_rata: Backdoor Roth: pro-rata rule applies if pre-tax IRA balance exists
view.s219.nd.spousal_separate: Spousal basis tracked separately on each return
view.s221.h1.title: // § 221 STUDENT LOAN INTEREST
view.s221.hint.intro: Above-the-line deduction (Schedule 1) up to $2,500/year per RETURN. MAGI phase-out 2024: $80k-$95k single / $165k-$195k MFJ. MFS NOT allowed. Cannot be claimed if you're a DEPENDENT on someone else's return. Loan must be qualified — for taxpayer / spouse / dependent's education. Voluntary and capitalized interest both count.
view.s221.label.dependent: Claimed as dependent on another return?
view.s221.note.secure: Effective 2024: employers can MATCH employee student loan payments to 401(k) / 403(b) plan. Treated like deferral for match purposes — even if employee makes no plan contribution. Helps young workers debt-paydown + retirement save simultaneously.
view.s221.qual.education: Money used SOLELY to pay qualified education expenses
view.s221.qual.refinanced: Refinanced student loans: maintain qualified character if proceeds used only to pay original
view.s221.qual.related: NOT from related person (parent), employer benefit, or qualified employer plan
view.s221.qual.timing: Loan must be primarily liable on (cosigners may share)
view.s221.qual.who: For taxpayer / spouse / dependent at time loan taken out
view.s221.warning.ineligible: Not eligible: MFS filers + dependents on another return are disqualified.
view.s23.h1.title: // § 23 ADOPTION CREDIT
view.s23.hint.intro: Up to $16,810 (2024) credit per child for qualified adoption expenses. MAGI phase-out: $252,150-$292,150 (2024). 5-yr carryforward (non-refundable). Special needs adoption: FULL credit regardless of expenses incurred. § 137: employer-paid adoption assistance up to $16,810 excluded from income (separate, NOT double-dip). Form 8839.
view.s23.qual.illegal: NOT: expenses violating state law / public policy
view.s23.qual.reimbursed: NOT: expenses reimbursed by employer / charity / gov't program
view.s23.qual.travel: Travel + lodging + meals (while traveling for adoption)
view.s23.rel.actc: § 24 Additional CTC: refundable portion up to $1,700 (2024)
view.s23.rel.cdcc: § 21 Child + Dependent Care Credit: 20-35% on up to $3k/$6k expenses
view.s23.rel.ctc: § 24 Child Tax Credit: $2,000 per qualifying child < 17
view.s23.sn.factors: Factors: ethnic background, age, sibling group, medical/emotional condition
view.s23.sn.harder_to_place: State determines specific factor makes child harder to place
view.s23.sn.state_determined: State determines child cannot return to parents' home
view.s23.sn.tax_id: Use Adoption Taxpayer ID Number (ATIN) before SSN issued
view.s23.sn.title_iv_e: Title IV-E foster care payments don't reduce credit
view.s23.sn.us_citizen: US citizen or resident at time adoption efforts began
view.s24.h1.title: // § 24 CHILD TAX CREDIT
view.s24.hint.intro: 2024 CTC $2,000/child under 17, $1,700 refundable ACTC. $500 ODC for other deps. MAGI phase $200K/$400K. SSN required. Sunsets to $1K post-2025.
view.s245A.h1.title: // § 245A 100% DRD (Participation)
view.s245A.hint.intro: 100% deduction for foreign-source portion of dividends from 10%-owned foreign corp ('specified 10% owned'). Holding period: 365 / 731 days (45 / 90 for preferred). Hybrid dividend rule (§ 245A(e)): deduction DENIED if foreign payor deducted it. § 1248 gain on CFC stock sale recharacterized to E&P → eligible for § 245A. Net effect: US C-corps largely exempt from tax on actual foreign dividends.
view.s245A.req.holding: Holding period: 365 days within 731-day window (45 / 90 days for preferred)
view.s245A.req.no_deduction: Expenses allocable to deductible dividend NOT deductible (no whipsaw)
view.s245A.req.no_ftc: No § 901 FTC + no § 902 indirect credit on excluded portion (logical — no income to credit against)
view.s245A.req.no_hybrid: Not a hybrid dividend (would create double non-tax)
view.s245A.req.not_pfic: Foreign payor cannot be PFIC for any year of US shareholder ownership
view.s245A.req.specified10: 'Specified 10% owned' foreign corp: 10%+ by vote OR value
view.s245A.req.us_corp: Recipient must be US C-corp (no S-corp, no individual)
view.s245A.s1248.basic: CFC stock sale: gain up to allocated E&P recharacterized as DIVIDEND under § 1248
view.s245A.s1248.deconsolidation: Trumps gain on CFC sale → enables tax-free repatriation via sale
view.s245A.s1248.deduction: Recharacterized portion eligible for § 245A 100% DRD (huge planning win)
view.s245A.s1248.requires: Still requires holding period + foreign source + not hybrid
view.s245A.s1248_note: § 1248 recharacterized gain captured under § 245A — full DRD on E&P portion. Net result: tax-free repatriation via CFC sale. Coordinate with § 367 outbound transfer rules.
view.s248.elect.amortization_begin: Amortization begins month business begins (active business start)
view.s248.elect.default: DEFAULT election: deemed elected if return reports per § 248 treatment
view.s248.elect.election_capitalize: Election to CAPITALIZE entire amount (no current deduction): irrevocable
view.s248.elect.no_form: No separate election form — choice made on first return
view.s248.elect.short_period: Short first year: prorated based on months
view.s248.eligible.accounting: Accounting fees: setting up books + records + first audit
view.s248.eligible.federal_filing: Federal filing fees: EIN, S-corp Form 2553, exempt org Form 1023
view.s248.eligible.legal: Legal fees: incorporation, drafting articles + bylaws, organizing meetings
view.s248.eligible.organizational_meetin: Organizational meetings + temporary directors / officers compensation
view.s248.eligible.printing: Printing of stock certificates, corporate seals, minutes book
view.s248.eligible.registered_agent: Registered agent / resident agent setup fees
view.s248.eligible.state_filing: State filing fees: Articles of Incorporation, Statement of Information
view.s248.h1.title: // § 248 ORGANIZATIONAL EXPENDITURES
view.s248.hint.intro: $5K immediate deduction in first tax year + remaining amortized over 180 months (15 yrs). Phaseout: $5K reduces $-for-$ when org exp > $50K → no immediate at $55K. Parallel: § 195 startup + § 709 partnership organizational. Eligible expenses: legal fees, accounting fees, state filing fees, incorporation costs. NOT eligible: stock issuance costs, transfer of assets, lobbying. Election: made on first return; no separate form (Reg § 1.248-1).
view.s248.ineligible.stock_issuance: Stock issuance costs: § 263(a) capital expenditure — reduce stock proceeds
view.s248.ineligible.transfer_assets: Transfer of assets to new corp: separate § 351 / § 1032 treatment
view.s248.phaseout_note: Organizational expenditures EXCEED $55K phaseout — NO immediate $5K deduction. Entire amount must be amortized over 180 months. Common for sophisticated corporate structures with extensive legal, accounting, securities counsel work. Consider whether to fully capitalize to basis instead (election made on first return).
view.s250.fdii.deemed: Deemed Intangible Income = DEI − 10% × Domestic QBAI
view.s250.fdii.dei: DEI = Deduction Eligible Income (gross income less subF, GILTI, dividends, etc.)
view.s250.fdii.documentation: Documentation required: sales contracts + delivery proof + foreign-use evidence
view.s250.fdii.fdii: FDII = (DEII / DEI) × DEII (Foreign-Derived Income from Eligible Use)
view.s250.fdii.foreign_pct: Foreign-use fraction = sales to foreign / total sales (services or sales)
view.s250.fdii.related_party: Related-party sales to non-US end user qualifies if documented to ultimate use
view.s250.h1.title: // § 250 FDII + GILTI DEDUCTION
view.s250.hint.intro: FDII = 37.5% deduction → ~13.125% effective rate (TCJA carrot to keep IP in US). GILTI = 50% deduction → ~10.5% effective rate (anti-deferral counterweight). Post-2025: FDII drops to 21.875% (16.4% effective); GILTI drops to 37.5% (13.125% effective). Limitation: deduction ≤ taxable income (no NOL). C-corps only (and § 962-electing individuals).
view.s250.qual.commodities: Commodity trading + financial product trading excluded
view.s250.qual.goods: Tangible property sold for foreign use (manufactured + customized + warranty)
view.s250.qual.intangibles: Licenses + royalties on IP used outside US
view.s250.qual.related_us: NOT qualifying: sales to foreign related party for US-end use
view.s250.qual.services: Services performed FOR foreign person (delivery point matters)
view.s250.ti_cap_note: Taxable income limitation triggered: full uncapped deduction exceeds TI. Deduction reduced to prevent NOL creation. Unused portion does NOT carry forward — lost permanently.
view.s2518.fail.cost_basis: Disclaimant becomes property owner — § 1014 step-up still applies
view.s2518.fail.deemed_gift: Treated as accepted + new gift from disclaimant to next beneficiary
view.s2518.fail.gift_tax: § 2511 gift tax applies — file Form 709 + use lifetime exemption
view.s2518.fail.late_filing: 9-month deadline is strict — no equitable tolling
view.s2518.fail.partial_consequences: Partial disclaimer with conditions = total failure (not partial)
view.s2518.fail.state_law_only: State-law disclaimer may still effective for state purposes
view.s2518.failed_note: Disclaimer FAILS § 2518 — treated as accepted + gift from disclaimant. Triggers § 2511 gift tax. State-law disclaimer may still effective for non-tax purposes.
view.s2518.five.passes: Passes WITHOUT disclaimant direction (governing instrument controls)
view.s2518.five.timing: 9 months from later of: (a) date of transfer creating interest, OR (b) age 21 for minor
view.s2518.five.unconditional: UNCONDITIONAL + irrevocable — no conditions, no power to revoke
view.s2518.five.written: WRITTEN refusal — signed by disclaimant or legal rep
view.s2518.h1.title: // § 2518 QUALIFIED DISCLAIMER
view.s2518.hint.intro: Qualified disclaimer = refusal to accept inheritance. 5 reqs: written + 9 months + unconditional + no benefit + passes without disclaimant direction. NOT a gift under § 2511.
view.s2518.special.fmv_date: FMV at date of disclaimer (not date of original transfer)
view.s2518.special.formula: Formula clause disclaimer permitted (e.g., applicable exclusion)
view.s2518.special.ira_separate: IRA / retirement: each beneficiary disclaims separately
view.s2518.special.partial_undivided: Partial disclaimer: must be UNDIVIDED interest (severable share)
view.s2518.special.power_of_appointment: General power of appointment may be disclaimed
view.s2518.special.spouse_exception: Otherwise, disclaimed interest CANNOT pass to disclaimant
view.s2518.special.spouse_redirect: § 2518(b)(4)(A) — surviving spouse may disclaim AND interest passes to spouse
view.s2518.state.adoption: Adopted in 40+ states (CA, NY, IL, TX vary)
view.s2518.state.no_time_limit: UDPIA: NO state law time limit (federal 9-month controls for tax)
view.s2518.state.udpia: Uniform Disclaimer of Property Interests Act (UDPIA, 2002)
view.s2518.state.unborn: Unborn beneficiary: virtual representation may apply
view.s2518.uc.bypass_trust: Disclaimer to bypass trust funding (reduces surviving spouse's estate)
view.s2518.uc.creditor: Creditor protection: disclaimed property never owned by disclaimant
view.s2518.uc.elderly: Elderly child / financially independent — disclaim in favor of next gen
view.s2518.uc.poor_planning: Post-mortem correction of pre-mortem planning errors
view.s2518.uc.qtip_election: Disclaim to enable QTIP election + reverse direction
view.s2518.uc.skip_generation: Disclaimer to skip generation (children disclaim → grandchildren — GST issues)
view.s25D.carry_note: Unused credit carries forward INDEFINITELY — Roth conversion, IRA distribution, or other taxable event can use credit. § 25D is non-refundable but never expires. Unlike § 38 GBC (20-yr limit), § 25D has no time limit on carryforward.
view.s25D.elig.battery_storage: Battery storage ≥ 3 kWh — added IRA 2022 as standalone (no solar pairing required)
view.s25D.elig.financing: Financing costs (interest) NOT in basis; deductible separately
view.s25D.elig.fuel_cell: Fuel cell: $500/0.5 kW capacity limit (max $1,667 / household per yr × 30% = $500)
view.s25D.elig.geothermal: Geothermal heat pump: Energy Star certified; loop + heat exchanger + pump
view.s25D.elig.labor: Labor + installation INCLUDED in credit basis
view.s25D.elig.roof: NOT eligible: structural roof reinforcement, general roof replacement (unless integrated PV)
view.s25D.elig.solar_pv: Solar PV: panels, inverters, wiring, mounting, installation labor
view.s25D.elig.solar_thermal: Solar thermal water heating: NOT pool / hot tub heating
view.s25D.elig.wind: Small wind: ≤ 100 kW capacity, residential property
view.s25D.h1.title: // § 25D RESIDENTIAL CLEAN ENERGY
view.s25D.hint.intro: 30% credit on residential property: solar PV, solar thermal water, fuel cells, geothermal heat pump, small wind, battery storage ≥ 3 kWh. IRA 2022 extended through 2034 (26% in 2033, 22% in 2034); 30% through 2032. Battery storage added 2023 as standalone qualified (no solar pairing). Non-refundable but UNLIMITED carryforward (unused offsets future tax). Form 5695. Principal residence + 2nd home eligible; rentals NO. Includes labor + installation.
view.s25D.opt.basis_reduce: Basis reduction: 50% — applies to depreciation IF business use portion
view.s25D.opt.battery_alone: Standalone battery now qualifies — install without solar if grid backup desired
view.s25D.opt.battery_sizing: Battery 3+ kWh: avoid sub-3 kWh batteries (no credit)
view.s25D.opt.bundling: Bundle with state + utility rebates — § 25D applies to cost AFTER rebates (federal subtracts state)
view.s25D.opt.divided_ownership: Co-owners: each claims proportionate share based on % paid
view.s25D.opt.no_lease: Solar lease / PPA: NOT eligible — only OWNED systems qualify
view.s25D.opt.tax_planning: Coordinate with income year to use credit (Roth conv, capital gains realization)
view.s25E.elig.age: Model year at least 2 years older than current year
view.s25E.elig.battery_phev: PHEV: battery ≥ 7 kWh; BEV: no battery min stated; FCV: any
view.s25E.elig.dealer: Sold by IRS-registered dealer (NOT private party)
view.s25E.elig.first_resale: FIRST resale after original sale (not flippers / repeat sellers)
view.s25E.elig.income: MAGI: $75K / $112.5K / $150K (CURRENT or PRIOR yr — pick lower)
view.s25E.elig.no_domestic: NO domestic assembly / minerals / battery components requirement
view.s25E.elig.no_personal_use_before: Not used by purchasing taxpayer before
view.s25E.elig.price: Sale price ≤ $25,000 (includes options, dealer prep, document fees)
view.s25E.ex.ford_lightning: Used Ford Lightning (2022, $30K): NO CREDIT — over $25K cap
view.s25E.ex.mach_e: Used Mach-E (2022, $26K): NO CREDIT — over $25K cap
view.s25E.ex.prime: Used Prius Prime PHEV (2020, $18K): credit min($4,000, $5,400) = $4,000
view.s25E.ex.private_party: Private party sale (no dealer): NO CREDIT — must be qualified dealer
view.s25E.h1.title: // § 25E USED CLEAN VEHICLE
view.s25E.hint.intro: 30% of sale price OR $4,000 — LOWER applies. Sale price ≤ $25,000. Vehicle must be at least 2 model years older than current. MAGI limit: $75K single / $112.5K HoH / $150K MFJ. First resale only (not flippers). Qualified dealer required (registered with IRS Energy Credits Online). Buyer transfers credit to dealer at POS. One-per-three-year limit for buyer. Form 8936. NO domestic assembly / minerals / battery components requirements — unlike § 30D.
view.s25E.over_price_note: Sale price exceeds $25,000 cap. Negotiate price down or look at lower-priced inventory. For higher-priced new vehicles, consider § 30D (new) or § 45W (commercial / lease loophole) instead. § 25E is targeted at affordability for used EV market.
view.s25E.tx.dealer_3rd_party: 3rd-party finance ok; dealer must process credit transfer
view.s25E.tx.dealer_advance: Dealer receives advance payment from IRS (Form 8300)
view.s25E.tx.discount: Discount applied at POS — dealer registers via IRS Energy Credits Online
view.s25E.tx.no_clawback_safe_harbor: No clawback if buyer not aware of excess MAGI
view.s25E.tx.refundable_effect: Refundable effect: even if no tax liability, buyer gets discount
